title: atriplex hastata subsp. triangularis
intro: Atriplex hastata subsp. triangularis, also known as orache or mountain spinach, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Amaranthaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive system function, as well as providing essential minerals and vitamins. Atriplex hastata subsp. triangularis can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, and is often consumed raw in salads or cooked as a leafy green vegetable.
